title: rubus × clypeatus
intro: Rubus × clypeatus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a hybrid plant species that combines the characteristics of the American thimbleberry (Rubus parviflorus) and the red raspberry (Rubus idaeus). The thimbleberry has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its high content of salicylic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Rubus × clypeatus include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, improve digestion, and support cardiovascular health. The herbal preparations of thimbleberry include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
